tasks.title: Pre-Migration Setup and Environment Preparation
tasks.description: Create migration branch, install Expo CLI tools, setup development environment, and prepare for Expo SDK 51 migration
tasks.details:
1. Create 'expo-migration' branch from main
2. Install Expo CLI globally: npm install -g expo-cli@latest eas-cli@latest
3. Create full project backup (git stash or separate directory)
4. Login to Expo account: expo login
5. Verify Node.js version >=18.0.0
6. Setup .env.local for development
7. Document current bundle identifiers and app configurations
8. IMPORTANT: Follow MIGRATION-GUIDE.md Section 1 - Initial Setup (30 minutes) for detailed step-by-step instructions

Reference Document: MIGRATION-GUIDE.md Section 1
tasks.testStrategy: Verify Expo CLI installation with 'expo --version' and 'eas --version', confirm login status with 'expo whoami', ensure git branch created successfully, validate Node.js version compatibility. Follow the automated steps in MIGRATION-GUIDE.md Section 1

subtasks.title: Create Migration Branch and Project Backup
subtasks.description: Create the expo-migration branch from main, stash any uncommitted changes, and create a complete project backup for rollback safety
subtasks.details: Execute: git checkout main && git pull origin main && git checkout -b expo-migration && git stash save 'Pre-migration changes' && cp -r . ../wildlife-watcher-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S). Document the backup location in a migration-log.txt file. Ensure all current work is safely preserved before beginning migration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify branch creation with git branch -a, confirm backup directory exists and contains all project files, check git stash list shows saved changes if any existed

subtasks.title: Install and Configure Expo CLI Tools
subtasks.description: Install the latest Expo CLI and EAS CLI globally, verify Node.js compatibility, and authenticate with Expo account
subtasks.details: Install tools: npm install -g expo-cli@latest eas-cli@latest. Verify Node.js version: node --version (must be >=18.0.0). If Node version is insufficient, update via nvm or system package manager. Login to Expo: expo login (have credentials ready). Store login status for team reference.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run expo --version and eas --version to confirm installation, execute expo whoami to verify authentication, run node --version to confirm >=18.0.0

subtasks.title: Document Current App Configuration
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation of current bundle identifiers, app configurations, and environment variables before migration
subtasks.details: Extract and document: 1) Current bundle IDs from android/app/build.gradle (applicationId) and ios/WildlifeWatcherMobileApp/Info.plist (CFBundleIdentifier), 2) App display names from app.json, 3) All environment variables from .env files, 4) Current version numbers and build numbers. Create migration-config-snapshot.json with all values. This ensures we can reference original configurations during migration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify migration-config-snapshot.json contains all bundle IDs, app names, version numbers, and lists all environment variables currently in use

subtasks.title: Setup Development Environment Configuration
subtasks.description: Create and configure .env.local for development, ensuring all necessary environment variables are properly set for Expo development
subtasks.details: Create .env.local file copying from .env.example or existing .env. Add Expo-specific variables if needed (EXPO_PUBLIC_ prefix for client-side vars). Ensure all API keys, endpoints, and configuration values are present. Update .gitignore to exclude .env.local. Document any new Expo-specific environment variables that will be needed.

subtasks.title: Complete Required Human Actions for Expo Project Setup
subtasks.description: Execute the human-required actions from MIGRATION-GUIDE.md including Expo login, project creation, and PoC file copying to prepare for Section 2
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-07-28T05:49:40.467Z>
This task covers all the critical human actions required from MIGRATION-GUIDE.md before proceeding to Section 2. Based on the guide, the specific actions needed are:

1. **Expo Account Login** (Lines 86-90):
   - Run `eas login` command
   - Enter Expo account credentials when prompted
   - Verify authentication with `expo whoami`

2. **Create Expo Project** (Lines 92-102):
   - Option 1: Run `eas project:create --name="Wildlife Watcher Expo"`
   - Option 2: Use web dashboard at https://expo.dev/accounts/[your-account]/projects
   - Project name: "Wildlife Watcher Expo"
   - Project slug: "wildlife-watcher-expo"
   - Verify project creation successful

3. **Copy PoC Reference Files** (Lines 17-22, 270-274):
   - Copy `/ww-expo-poc/scripts/` entire folder to current directory
   - Copy `/ww-expo-poc/app.config.js` (as reference)
   - Copy `/ww-expo-poc/eas.json` (as reference)
   - Copy `/project-context/connecting-android-phone-instructions/` folder
   - Create `scripts/` directory if needed

4. **Prepare Prerequisites for Section 2**:
   - Verify Google Maps API keys are available
   - Confirm Android device with developer mode enabled is ready
   - Ensure Apple Developer account access (if doing iOS)
   - Validate all files copied correctly

This task gates progression to Section 2 (Core Expo Integration) and must be completed by human before automated execution can continue.
</info added on 2025-07-28T05:49:40.467Z>

subtasks.title: Replace File System Module
subtasks.description: Replace react-native-fs with expo-file-system@~16.0.0 and update all file operations
subtasks.details:
Install expo-file-system@~16.0.0 using 'npm install expo-file-system@~16.0.0'. Then uninstall react-native-fs with 'npm uninstall react-native-fs'. Update all imports from 'react-native-fs' to 'expo-file-system' throughout the codebase. Reference FILE-SYSTEM-MIGRATION-EXAMPLES.md for API conversion patterns. Common replacements include: RNFS.readFile() to FileSystem.readAsStringAsync(), RNFS.writeFile() to FileSystem.writeAsStringAsync(), RNFS.exists() to FileSystem.getInfoAsync(). Update any file path constants to use FileSystem.documentDirectory or FileSystem.cacheDirectory.
<info added on 2025-07-28T11:52:05.787Z>
COMPLETED: Successfully replaced react-native-fs with expo-file-system. Migrated DfuScreen.tsx file system operations: RNFS.CachesDirectoryPath to FileSystem.cacheDirectory, RNFS.copyFile to FileSystem.copyAsync, RNFS.unlink to FileSystem.deleteAsync with idempotent option. Package uninstalled successfully. No remaining react-native-fs imports found in source code. NOTE: Unit tests for DfuScreen file system operations should be added as follow-up task.
</info added on 2025-07-28T11:52:05.787Z>

tasks.details:
FOUNDATION LAYER - Task 11: Offline SQLite Foundation

This task creates the complete offline-first data management system that enables the Wildlife Watcher app to function fully offline during field deployments and sync when connectivity is restored, implementing organisation multi-tenancy and role-based data access patterns per Implementation Spec v1.4.6.

## Implementation Requirements:

### **ENHANCED** 11.1: SQLite Database Setup with Multi-Tenancy
- Initialize expo-sqlite database (`wildlife_watcher.db`)
- **NEW**: Organisation-scoped database schemas
- **NEW**: User role-based data access patterns
- Create schema for offline operations:
  - `offline_queue` table for sync operations
  - `local_deployments` table for offline deployment storage
  - `local_projects` table for offline project storage (organisation-scoped)
  - `local_devices` table for device information cache
  - `local_organisations` table for organisation data
  - `local_user_roles` table for role-based access control
- Migration system for schema updates
- Database connection management

### **ENHANCED** 11.2: Offline Service Architecture with Role-Based Sync
- Create `src/services/offline/OfflineService.ts` with organisation-aware operations
- **NEW**: Maestro test-driven implementation for offline service layer
- Queue system for offline operations with organisation scoping
- **NEW**: Role-based sync filtering:
  - `ww_admin`: Global sync across all organisations and data
  - `project_admin`: Organisation-scoped sync for managed projects
  - `project_member`: Project-scoped sync for accessible projects only
- **NEW**: Organisation context for all offline operations and data isolation
- **NEW**: LoRaWAN status field sync (battery_level, sd_card_usage) with offline caching
- Network state monitoring with organisation priority handling
- Conflict resolution strategies with user role validation
- Retry logic for failed operations with exponential backoff
- Operation deduplication with organisation-based filtering
- **NEW**: Claude Flow SPARC integration for offline architecture patterns

### 11.3: Sync Infrastructure
- Background sync when network available
- Incremental sync to minimize data transfer
- Data integrity validation
- Sync status indicators
- Sync priority system (critical operations first)
- Partial sync failure handling

### **NEW** 11.4: WW Admin Offline Features (MVP User Provisioning)
- **WW Admin User Provisioning**: Offline access to user management across organisations
- Model management offline sync (species, brands, models) for system configuration
- Organisation user list caching with role-based filtering
- **NEW**: User role assignment offline capability (ww_admin, project_admin, project_member)
- System configuration offline access for WW Admin users
- Global admin data synchronization with organisation isolation
- **NEW**: LoRaWAN device configuration offline sync for admin users
- **NEW**: Cross-organisation analytics and reporting offline access
- **NEW**: Bulk user operations offline queue (invite, role changes, deactivation)

### 11.5: Integration Patterns
- Offline-first service layer with automatic queuing
- Integration with Redux slices for seamless state management
- Offline indicators throughout UI
- Optimistic UI updates with rollback capability

## Dependencies:
- Task 10: Core Redux Integration (for state management integration)
- expo-sqlite package (~13.4.0)
- @react-native-community/netinfo for network monitoring
- Supabase client for sync operations

## Technical Patterns:
- **NEW**: Follow Claude Flow SPARC methodology for offline service architecture
- Follow offline service patterns from API Integration Guide with organisation scoping
- **NEW**: Implement Maestro TDD patterns for all offline service operations
- Implement BaseService pattern for consistent error handling with role validation
- **NEW**: Use generated Supabase TypeScript types with organisation and user role extensions
- Follow conflict resolution patterns from MVP2 docs with user role consideration
- **NEW**: Implement organisation data isolation patterns throughout offline layer
- **NEW**: Role-based data access patterns (RLS policy enforcement offline)

## Key Features:
- **Complete Offline Functionality**: All core operations work without network
- **Intelligent Sync**: Only sync changed data, handle conflicts gracefully
- **Network Resilience**: Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- **Data Integrity**: Validation and rollback mechanisms
- **User Experience**: Clear offline/sync status indicators

## Testing Requirements:
- Unit tests for OfflineService and database operations
- Integration tests for sync scenarios
- Test offline/online transition scenarios
- Test conflict resolution with multiple device changes
- Test data integrity after various sync failure scenarios
- Performance tests with large offline operation queues

## Success Criteria:
- [ ] SQLite database operational with proper schema
- [ ] Offline operations queued correctly and persistently
- [ ] Sync system functional with network state changes
- [ ] No data loss during offline/online transitions
- [ ] Performance acceptable on low-end devices
- [ ] Conflict resolution working for simultaneous edits
- [ ] Background sync operating without blocking UI
- [ ] Proper error handling and user feedback

PARALLEL DEVELOPMENT STREAM B - Task 16: End Deployment Flow

This task implements the deployment termination workflow that enables users to properly end active wildlife monitoring deployments and retrieve final data.

## Implementation Requirements:

### 16.1: End Deployment Screen
- Create `src/navigation/screens/deployment/end/EndDeploymentScreen.tsx`
- **Active Deployment Selection**: List user's active deployments with filtering
- **Deployment Information Display**: Show deployment details, duration, location
- **Device Status Check**: Display current device connectivity and battery status
- **Termination Confirmation**: Clear confirmation dialog with deployment impact info
- **Batch Operations**: Option to end multiple deployments simultaneously

### 16.2: Device Reconnection & Communication
- **Automatic Device Discovery**: Attempt to reconnect to deployment device
- **Manual Device Selection**: If automatic fails, allow manual device selection
- **BLE Communication**: Send end deployment command to device
- **Device Status Retrieval**: Get final status data (battery, storage, logs)
- **Connection Fallback**: Handle scenarios where device is unreachable
- **Timeout Handling**: Graceful handling of communication timeouts

### 16.3: End Deployment Service Integration
- Create deployment termination service extending BaseService
- **Status Update to Supabase**: Mark deployment as ended with timestamp
- **Final Data Collection**: Retrieve and store final device metrics
- **Cleanup Operations**: Remove active deployment from local cache
- **Data Integrity**: Ensure deployment data is preserved correctly
- **Offline Support**: Queue termination if device is offline

### 16.4: Completion & Feedback
- **Success Confirmation**: Clear feedback that deployment was ended successfully
- **Data Summary**: Show final deployment statistics and duration
- **Next Steps**: Options to view deployment details or start new deployment
- **Error Recovery**: Handle partial failures and provide recovery options
- **Navigation**: Return to appropriate screen (Maps or Deployments list)

## Dependencies:
- Task 15: Start Deployment Flow (for deployment context)
- Existing BLE functionality for device communication
- Supabase integration for deployment status updates
- Offline queue system for reliable updates

## Technical Patterns:
- Follow Component Implementation Patterns for consistent UI
- Use LoadingStates pattern for device communication
- Implement ConfirmationDialog for termination confirmation
- Follow BaseService pattern for reliable API operations
- Use proper error handling and user feedback

## Key Features:
- **Streamlined Termination**: Simple process to end active deployments
- **Device Communication**: Proper communication with field devices
- **Data Preservation**: Ensure all deployment data is captured and stored
- **Offline Support**: End deployments even without network connectivity
- **Batch Operations**: Efficiency features for managing multiple deployments
- **Error Recovery**: Robust handling of device communication failures

## Workflow Steps:
1. **Select Deployment** → Choose active deployment to end
2. **Device Connection** → Reconnect to deployment device
3. **Final Data Retrieval** → Get final status and metrics
4. **Termination Confirmation** → Confirm deployment end
5. **Status Update** → Update deployment status in backend
6. **Completion Feedback** → Provide success confirmation and options

## Integration Points:
- Maps screen provides "End Deployment" quick action
- Deployments list screen allows batch termination
- Device management screen shows deployment status
- Real-time updates reflect termination across app

## Testing Requirements:
- Integration tests for complete end deployment workflow
- Unit tests for deployment termination service
- Test BLE device communication and reconnection
- Test offline termination scenarios
- Test error handling for unreachable devices
- Test batch termination functionality
- Test data integrity during termination process

## Success Criteria:
- [ ] End deployment flow working smoothly from selection to completion
- [ ] Device communication reliable with proper error handling
- [ ] Deployment status updates synchronized correctly
- [ ] Offline termination working with sync when network available
- [ ] Batch operations functional for multiple deployments
- [ ] Clear user feedback throughout the termination process
- [ ] Data integrity maintained during all termination scenarios
- [ ] Error recovery mechanisms handling edge cases effectively

tasks.details:
PARALLEL DEVELOPMENT STREAM C - Task 20: Offline Synchronization System

This task completes the offline-first architecture by implementing comprehensive synchronization capabilities that ensure data integrity and seamless user experience across online/offline transitions.

## Implementation Requirements:

### 20.1: Complete Sync Service Implementation
- Extend `src/services/offline/OfflineService.ts` with full synchronization logic
- **Bidirectional Sync**: Handle both local-to-remote and remote-to-local data flow
- **Incremental Sync**: Only sync changed data to minimize bandwidth usage
- **Priority-based Sync**: Critical operations (deployments, device status) sync first
- **Batch Operations**: Group related operations for efficient network usage
- **Transaction Management**: Ensure data consistency during sync operations
- **Dependency Resolution**: Handle data dependencies during sync (projects before deployments)

### 20.2: Advanced Conflict Resolution
- Create `src/services/offline/ConflictResolver.ts` for intelligent conflict handling
- **Conflict Detection**: Identify data conflicts based on timestamps and versions
- **Resolution Strategies**:
  - Last-write-wins for most data types
  - Merge strategies for project member lists
  - Deployment status conflicts (ended status always wins)
  - User-guided resolution for critical conflicts
- **Data Validation**: Ensure resolved data meets integrity constraints
- **Conflict Logging**: Track and report conflict resolution for debugging

### 20.3: Sync UI and Status Management
- Create sync status indicators throughout the app
- **Global Sync Status**: Persistent indicator showing sync state
- **Sync Progress**: Real-time progress for ongoing sync operations
- **Manual Sync Triggers**: User-initiated sync buttons with feedback
- **Sync History**: Log of recent sync operations and their outcomes
- **Error Reporting**: Clear error messages for sync failures with suggested actions
- **Sync Settings**: User preferences for sync frequency and behavior

### 20.4: Performance Optimization & Background Sync
- **Efficient Algorithms**: Optimize sync algorithms for speed and battery usage
- **Background Processing**: Sync operations that don't block UI interactions
- **Memory Management**: Efficient handling of large datasets during sync
- **Network Optimization**: Minimize network usage and handle poor connections
- **Battery Optimization**: Intelligent sync scheduling to preserve battery life
- **Storage Management**: Efficient local storage usage and cleanup

## Dependencies:
- Task 11: Offline SQLite Foundation (base offline architecture)
- All data services (Projects, Deployments, Devices) for sync integration
- Network monitoring and Supabase client for remote operations
- Background task management for non-blocking sync

## Technical Patterns:
- Follow offline service patterns from API Integration Guide
- Implement conflict resolution patterns from MVP2 docs
- Use proper TypeScript typing for all sync operations
- Follow performance optimization guidelines
- Use background task patterns for React Native

## Key Features:
- **Intelligent Synchronization**: Smart sync algorithms that minimize data transfer
- **Robust Conflict Resolution**: Handle data conflicts gracefully without data loss
- **User-Friendly Interface**: Clear sync status and control throughout the app
- **Performance Optimized**: Fast sync operations that don't impact user experience
- **Network Resilient**: Handle poor network conditions and intermittent connectivity
- **Battery Efficient**: Optimize sync operations for mobile device constraints

## Synchronization Features:
- **Delta Sync**: Only sync changes since last successful sync
- **Compression**: Compress sync payloads to reduce bandwidth usage
- **Retry Logic**: Exponential backoff for failed sync operations
- **Partial Sync**: Handle scenarios where only some data can be synced
- **Sync Validation**: Verify data integrity after sync operations
- **Recovery Mechanisms**: Recover from corrupted sync states

## User Experience Features:
- **Sync Status Indicators**: Always-visible sync status throughout app
- **Progress Feedback**: Real-time progress for long-running sync operations
- **Error Recovery**: User-friendly error handling with clear next steps
- **Manual Control**: User can trigger sync or pause automatic sync
- **Sync Insights**: Information about what was synced and when

## Integration Points:
- All CRUD operations integrate with sync queue
- Real-time UI updates during sync operations
- Conflict resolution UI appears when user intervention needed
- Background sync triggers based on network state changes
- Sync status affects user workflow (deployment creation, etc.)

## Testing Requirements:
- Unit tests for sync service and conflict resolution logic
- Integration tests for complete sync scenarios
- Performance tests with large datasets and poor network conditions
- Test conflict resolution with various data scenarios
- Test background sync operations and battery usage
- Test sync recovery from various failure modes
- Test user interface responsiveness during sync operations

## Success Criteria:
- [ ] Reliable offline/online synchronization with no data loss
- [ ] Conflict resolution working automatically and with user guidance
- [ ] Efficient sync operations with acceptable performance characteristics
- [ ] Clear user feedback and control over sync operations
- [ ] Robust error handling and recovery from sync failures
- [ ] Background sync operating without impacting user experience
- [ ] Sync system scales with large numbers of deployments and devices
- [ ] Battery and network usage optimized for mobile devices
tasks.testStrategy: Test bidirectional sync, verify conflict resolution, test performance with large datasets, validate background sync, test network failure recovery
